Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Howard Edward Babcock papers, 1907-1950.
Collection Number: 1618

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Howard Edward Babcock papers, 1907-1950.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
1618
Abstract:
Correspondence, mimeographed pamphlets and other printed material; photographs, and scrapbooks concerning Babcock's career.
Creator:
Babcock, H. E. (Howard Edward), 1889-1950.
Quanitities:
47.9 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Agricultural economist, administrator, and educator; farmer in Ithaca, New York.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Correspondence, mimeographed pamphlets and other printed material; photographs, and scrapbooks concerning Babcock's career as a county agent in Cattaraugus and Tompkins Counties, New York, as an executive in the New York State Farm Bureau, the Grange League Federation (G.L.F.) and related organizations (extensive), the New York State Agricultural Conference Board, the Federal Farm Board, Central Bank for Cooperatives, National Council of Farmer Cooperatives, and the New York State Emergency Food Commission, as a member or consultant of various New York State, Federal, and non-governmental boards and committees, and as professor of marketing and later trustee of Cornell University.
Includes correspondence with editors and publishers of numerous agricultural and other journals and with manufacturers of farm equipment, chiefly relating to agricultural research and education, to the promotion of effective farm organizations, and to state and national politics as they pertain to agriculture. Includes a "Unimal," a plastic toy creature designed by Karl Butler, representing five farm animals. Correspondents include Ezra Taft Benson, Edmund Ezra Day, Thomas E. Dewey, Victor Emanuel, Herbert H. Lehman, and Thomas E. Milliman.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Howard Edward Babcock papers, #1618. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Central Bank for Cooperatives
Cornell University. Board of Trustees
Cooperative Grange League Federation Exchange
National Council of Farmer Cooperatives
New York State College of Agriculture -- : Faculty.
New York State Conference Board of Farm Organizations
New York State Emergency Food Commission
New York State Farm Bureau Federation
United States. Federal Farm Board
Subjects:
Farmers.
College teachers.
Agriculturists.
Unimal.
Emergency food supply -- New York (State)
Agriculture -- Study and teaching -- New York (State)
Agriculture -- Research.
Agriculture -- Economic aspects.
Agriculture and politics.
Agricultural laws and legislation -- United States.
Agricultural laws and legislation -- New York (State)
Agricultural extension work -- New York (State) -- Tompkins County.
Agricultural extension work -- New York (State) -- Cattaraugus County.
Agricultural cooperative credit associations.
Agricultural administration.
Form and Genre Terms:
Scrapbooks.
Photographs.
